steps.title: ☀️ Solar Audification Study
steps.bodyHtml:
You will be listening to real spacecraft data and identifying interesting features. Please use headphones or high-quality speakers in a quiet environment free from distractions.

The data comes from NASA spacecraft and may contain gaps or sudden volume spikes. Please listen at a comfortable volume.

steps.title: About This Study
steps.bodyHtml:
Your task is to listen to magnetometer data from the GOES satellite and identify Electromagnetic Ion Cyclotron (EMIC) waves.

Please use headphones or high-quality speakers in a quiet environment free from distractions. Listen carefully and draw boxes around any features you identify in the spectrogram, and when you are finished use the "Complete" button to submit your results.

If you have any questions or need assistance, please contact the study coordinator, Lucy Williams, at lewilliams@smith.edu.

prompts.text: Listen carefully for periodic tonal patterns.
Draw boxes around any features you identify.
You can resize or delete features by clicking on them.

confirmCompletion.title: Complete?
confirmCompletion.message: Are you sure you're ready to finish? You won't be able to go back after this.

steps.title: 📋 Post-Study Questions
steps.bodyHtml: You will now be guided through a brief set of questions that should take 2–3 minutes.

steps.title: What is your background in physics or space science?

options.label: Minimal
options.description: Less than 1 year of coursework or experience

options.label: Considerable
options.description: 3–5 years of coursework or professional experience

options.label: Extensive
options.description: 5+ years of coursework or professional experience

steps.title: Have you previously analyzed scientific data?
steps.subtitle: (e.g., time series, spectrograms, satellite measurements)

options.label: Never
options.description: This was my first time working with scientific data

options.label: Rarely
options.description: A few times in coursework or casually

options.label: Occasionally
options.description: Regular coursework or some research involvement

options.label: Extensively
options.description: Multiple years of hands-on data analysis experience

steps.title: What is your level of musical experience?

options.label: Minimal
options.description: Some informal exposure (e.g., basic music classes in school)

options.label: Some
options.description: 1–3 years of musical training or playing an instrument

options.label: Considerable
options.description: 4+ years of training, active musician or regular performer

options.label: Extensive
options.description: Professional musician, music degree, or lifelong serious practice

steps.title: Do you have any additional feedback you'd like to share?
How did you learn about this experiment?
Thank You!
steps.bodyHtml:
Thank you for participating in this study! Your data has been saved.

If you have any questions, please contact Lucy Williams at lewilliams@smith.edu.
